strategic_priority: Develop and implement policies to foster food diversification and de-risking credit and insurance.
Promote production, consumption, preservation and trade of diversified and nutrition-adequate diets (especially plant-rich diets).

Focus on the implementation of a coherent, conducive legal, regulatory and institutional framework for plant seeds, animal breeds and fingerlings production, multiplication, distribution and marketing of the outputs.
Scale-up programs to promote and facilitate fortification or bio-fortification of widely consumed staple food.
Promote the utilisation and consumption of forgotten/orphan/indigenous foods.
Review and implement micronutrient, healthy diet guidelines and strategies.
Review and implement a SMART fertiliser Subsidy Programme to make it more focused, cost-effective and efficient.
Promote sustainable practices in agricultural production, good soil health, biodiversity and better land use options.
Climate-proofing food production and land use systems through climate change agriculture practices and insurance.
Promoting the use of diversified extension systems (VBA, MOA).
Promote implementation of a coherent, conducive legal, regulatory and institutional framework for natural resources and strengthening land tenure and governance.
Map land use potential for alternative land uses and relook at the land tenure system (long-term).
Boost nature-positive production.
Promote an integrated approach to natural resource management.
Undertake capacity building for communities to enable them to manage land use effectively.
Invest more in nutrition education and create SMART regulations for food safety without overburdening businesses, at both national and county levels.

Review and harmonise the current laws and regulations to address emerging challenges of food safety and provide for functional responsibilities along the food value chain at both national and county levels.

Develop and maintain modern physical market infrastructure (assembly or wholesale, retail and rural markets) with cold storage, cleaning, sorting and grading sheds, value addition and processing facilities for efficient marketing of agricultural produce.
Implement actions to reduce waste and losses in production and post-harvest operations.
Undertake sensitisation and awareness creation activities on FLW.
Implement actions to reduce waste and losses in production and post-harvest operations and undertake sensitisation and awareness creation activities on FLW.
Facilitate compliance to enhance compliance with set agricultural, food produce and products standards.
Enhance the capacity of surveillance institutions at national and county levels to enforce compliance with standards.
Harmonise and strengthen inter-agency efforts in food safety and quality control and monitoring to minimise overlaps and inter-agency conflicts.
Develop an FLW protocol and capacity-build stakeholders on reducing food loss and waste.

Invest in capacity building for youth (particularly in agribusiness), producers (extension system) and women along the value chains, and set up clear contract enforcement mechanisms that protect small-scale farmers.
Promote youth and women participation and entrepreneurship in the food and land use system.
Promote enhanced youth access to productive resources and inputs.
Promote women/youth-led food and land use research, innovation and technology adoption.
Promote access to markets and value addition for women/youth-led enterprises.
Strengthen youth and women leadership, capacity development and coordination in the food system.
Monitoring and evaluation of the action plan.
Coordination meetings for the action plan implementation.
